title: Forza Horizon 5

notes:
If you are using the Steam Flatpak from Flathub, Forza will complain that the default install directory Flathub Steam uses has "too long" of a path, as in, the game breaks if there are too many characters in the path to the install directory. To fix, I created a "Forza" folder in my home folder, changed the Flatpak permissions to give Steam access to it, added the folder as a game install location in Steam, and then moved the game files to that folder. 

Before I say this next part, keep in mind that I know how demanding this game is. I'm a glutton for punishment, trying to run games on underpowered hardware.

The next issue I encountered is that the game seems to have a coded-in crash whenever the graphics card is determined to be unsupported. The splash screen will show for a few seconds, but then, nothing. You can spoof your graphics card to a supported one by using dxvk config files, specifically, the "dxgi.customVendorId" and "dxgi.customDeviceId" options. Creating a file called "dxvk.conf" in the game directory and typing...

dxgi.customVendorId = xxxx

dxgi.customDeviceId = yyyy

...where xxxx is the vendor ID of a supported graphics card, and yyyy is the device ID, will allow you to bypass the crash and get the game to boot. However, once you try to actually play, the game will crash at the loading screen before any gameplay.

I have no evidence, but, if I had to guess, its likely due to a mismatch of expected GPU capabilities, based on the device ID fed to it, and actual GPU capabilities, causing it to get unexpected results.

Not sure if this is a Proton issue, or if the game crashes like this on Windows, too.

I know the very idea of playing the game on integrated graphics is a bit of a pipe dream, but I can usally get things working in even very demanding games with the game not crashing on me, as long as I set the graphics settings at the absolute lowest. Setting Forza's graphics at the lowest settings doesn't fix the crashes.
title: Forza Horizon 5
